summary: Reviewed recent ECG results — normal sinus rhythm. Blood pressure slightly elevated at 135/88. Discussed lifestyle modifications including reduced sodium intake and 30 minutes of daily walking. Prescribed low-dose antihypertensive. Follow-up recommended in 3 months.

chatHistory.text: I've reviewed your ECG. It looks mostly normal but I'd like to monitor your blood pressure closely.
Not at all. It's mild and very manageable with lifestyle changes. I'll send a prescription to your pharmacy.

summary: Patient reports recurring migraines 2–3 times per week for past month. Duration 4–6 hours each. Possible triggers identified: screen time, dehydration, irregular sleep. Recommended magnesium supplement and increased water intake. Prescribed sumatriptan for acute attacks.

chatHistory.text: Based on what you've described, these are classic tension-type migraines. Let's work on identifying your triggers.
I think it gets worse when I use my laptop for long hours.
That's very common. Take regular breaks every 30 minutes and use blue light glasses in the evenings.

notes: 3-month follow-up after blood pressure consultation. Doctor-assigned private session.

summary: Patient presented for routine general checkup. Blood pressure 120/80 mmHg, normal. Weight stable. No significant findings. Advised to maintain current diet and exercise routine.

chatHistory.text: Your vitals all look great today. Keep up the healthy habits!
